what_it_does: This file is a posted noticeboard note (a point/comment) by Tim in the dragnet-development channel, linked as a comment on item-760c7152, that records observations about the lack of channel-membership-based read access for dragnet findings and argues for configurable cross-channel visibility and search scope.
observations: The item type is 'note', source is 'claude_code', and state is 'posted'.
The author session is 'tim' and the channel is 'dragnet-development'.
It links to board://item-760c7152 with kind 'commented_on'.
It quotes a prior statement that there is no existing mechanism restricting dragnet finding read access to channel members.
It proposes that whether content is accessible to agents from other channels and what shows up in searches should be configurational.
It notes that agent data access is currently a limited problem because few agents are running and they run only for the author.
It cites Slack and Discord as partial inspirations for the channel model.
standouts: Identifies the gap that dragnet findings have no channel-membership read boundary.
Frames visibility across channels and search results as a configuration concern.
Acknowledges the current low urgency due to limited running agents, while still flagging it for future design.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item format with YAML frontmatter including id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, and history.
Uses a markdown body with a '[point]' marker and an inline quoted passage.
References another item via a structured 'commented_on' link.
concerns: The term 'configurational' is used without a concrete definition or mechanism.
The note mixes product design rationale with personal runtime context without stating a decision or next step.
notes: Tim records a design point that dragnet findings need channel-scoped, configurable read access and search visibility, inspired partly by chat apps.
questions: What exact access-control model is meant by 'configurational'?
Which agents or channels would be allowed or denied access under the proposed model?
What is the content of the linked item-760c7152?
How would the member-registry be extended to enforce read access rather than just delivery?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is titled and structured as a posted point/note about read-access design for dragnet findings.
apparent_themes: Agent data access and search scope
Extending the member-registry from routing to permissions
intention_signals: It appears to advocate for building a configuration layer that uses channel membership to govern which agents can read stored dragnet findings and what appears in searches, drawing on Slack/Discord-like channel semantics.
novelty: It reframes the member-registry from a message-delivery audience into a read-access control boundary, and connects that idea to both cross-channel agent visibility and search filtering.
contribution: Provides a user-level design rationale for channel-scoped read access, configurable cross-channel agent visibility, and search filtering for dragnet findings, and records Slack/Discord as a partial influence.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by Tim in dragnet-development commenting on the absence of channel-membership-based read access for dragnet findings and arguing for configurable cross-channel visibility and search scope.
intention_for_embedding: To record and advocate for a configurational access-control model in which channel membership determines which agents can read stored dragnet findings and what surfaces in searches.
